label: Numerical analysis

node.label: Numerical analysis
node.definition: Stable approximation, floating-point arithmetic, iterative solvers, and discretization make mathematical models computable.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Numerical analysis is to connect Calculus & automatic differentiation theory, Linear algebra to downstream capabilities including Differential equations, Scalars, vectors, matrices & tensors, Bayesian & approximate inference.
examples: substrate for Scalars, vectors, matrices & tensors
limitations: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting. Its direct dependencies include Calculus & automatic differentiation theory, Linear algebra.

dependencies.rationale: Numerical analysis rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Calculus & automatic differentiation theory.
Numerical analysis rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Linear algebra.

sentence_units.text: Stable approximation, floating-point arithmetic, iterative solvers, and discretization make mathematical models computable.
